name: Extract PID Checker Interface

tasks.title: Define PIDChecker interface and remove daemon import from validate
tasks.description: Define PIDChecker interface and remove daemon import from validate
tasks.body: Create a PIDChecker interface in validate (likely in a new file pid_checker.go or at the top of engine.go) with the five methods that validate actually uses from daemon.DaemonRepository: IsAlive() bool, PIDFileExists() bool, StopFileExists() bool, RemovePID() error, RemoveStopFile() error. Replace the *daemon.DaemonRepository field in Engine struct and all function parameters in fix.go with PIDChecker. Remove the daemon import from both engine.go and fix.go. Update the constructors NewEngine, NewEngineWithRepo, FixWithVerificationRepo, and ApplyDeterministicFixesRepo to accept PIDChecker instead of the concrete type. Update all callers in cmd/ that pass a *daemon.DaemonRepository to these functions (the concrete type satisfies the interface implicitly, so callers just need their type annotations adjusted if any). Verify go build ./... and go vet ./... pass cleanly. Run existing tests to confirm nothing regresses.
acceptance_criteria: validate package has zero imports of github.com/dorkusprime/wolfcastle/internal/daemon
PIDChecker interface is defined in the validate package with exactly the five methods: IsAlive

tasks.title: Add compile-time interface satisfaction check
tasks.body: Add a compile-time assertion in the daemon package (in repository.go or a dedicated file) that confirms *DaemonRepository satisfies validate.PIDChecker. Use the standard Go pattern: var _ validate.PIDChecker = (*DaemonRepository)(nil). This ensures future changes to either the interface or the struct produce a compile error rather than a silent runtime failure. Note: this import direction (daemon importing validate) is acceptable because validate is the interface owner and daemon is the implementor. If this creates an import cycle, place the assertion in an internal test file (repository_test.go) or a dedicated integration test instead.
acceptance_criteria: A compile-time assertion exists that *daemon.DaemonRepository satisfies validate.PIDChecker
go build ./... succeeds with the assertion in place

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Extract PID Checker Interface is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: grep internal/validate/*.go for any remaining import of daemon package
verify PIDChecker interface has exactly five methods matching the scope description
check that cmd/ callers still compile when passing *daemon.DaemonRepository where PIDChecker is expected

breadcrumbs.text: Extracted PIDChecker interface into pid_checker.go; replaced daemon.DaemonRepository with PIDChecker across engine.go and fix.go; validate production code has zero daemon imports

task-0001.objective: Define PIDChecker interface in validate to break the import dependency on daemon
task-0001.what_happened: Created internal/validate/pid_checker.go with the five-method PIDChecker interface. Replaced *daemon.DaemonRepository with PIDChecker in Engine struct, NewEngine, NewEngineWithRepo, FixWithVerification, FixWithVerificationRepo, ApplyDeterministicFixes, and ApplyDeterministicFixesRepo. Updated cmd/doctor.go and cmd/daemon/start.go to construct DaemonRepository and pass it as PIDChecker. Updated five test files to import daemon and wrap path strings.
went_well: The concrete type satisfies the interface implicitly, so no adapter code was needed
All 1.4s of validate tests pass, go build and go vet clean
improvements: The variadic PIDChecker pattern on convenience functions mirrors the old variadic string pattern; a separate constructor might be cleaner but would be a larger API change
action_items: The audit task should verify no daemon import leaks back into validate production code
